mineral.locality: Spain (Aragon — type locality), Morocco, USA, Czech Republic, Mexico, Namibia
mineral.key_physics: Orthorhombic polymorph of CaCO₃ — denser and harder than calcite. Metastable at surface conditions — slowly converts to calcite over geological time. Forms the shell material of most marine organisms. Flos Ferri = branching cave aragonite. Blue variety = Sr²⁺ substitution. Star clusters form radiating pseudohexagonal triplets.
mineral.notes: Named after Aragon, Spain. Polymorphs: calcite, vaterite. Biological aragonite = mollusk shells, coral skeletons.

light_color.primary_color: White, colorless, yellow, orange-brown, blue, green (variety dependent)
light_color.color_mechanism: Colorless/white = pure CaCO₃; yellow/orange = Fe²⁺/Fe³⁺; blue = Sr²⁺ substitution; green = Cu²⁺ traces
light_color.color_theory_note: H:250 is the blue-violet arc — the hue of deep sky at dusk, evoking ancestral memory, spiritual depth, and the passage of time. High lightness L:0.82 lifts it from heaviness into clarity — this is not the weight of the earth but the luminosity of the earth's breath. Low chroma C:0.08 gives it the quality of ancient stone rather than vivid color: presence measured in millennia, not moments.
light_color.fluorescence: Variable — blue, green, white under UV (locality dependent)

metaphysics.traditions: Powerful Earth-healer and grounding stone. Star cluster form radiates energy in all directions simultaneously. Connects to earth chakra and ancestral roots. Encourages patience, stability, and acceptance. Formed from marine life — carries memory of ancient oceans and biological evolution.
metaphysics.gaia_notes: Biological memory stone — forms the shells of living ocean creatures, a literal biological archive. AnchorPrism primary Earth-grounding node. Star cluster geometry maps to GAIA's radial energy distribution model.

warnings: Star cluster formations are fragile — handle with care

mineral.locality: Greenland, Russia (Kola Peninsula), Canada, South Africa
mineral.key_physics: Sodium-iron amphibole. Deep black with striking blue-silver chatoyant flash from oriented amphibole fibers. Fe²⁺/Fe³⁺ ratio controls optical properties. Often confused with Astrophyllite or Nuummite — distinct minerals. The blue flash is an orientation effect from parallel fibrous crystal inclusions.
mineral.notes: Named after Johan August Arfwedson (discoverer of lithium). IMA-recognized amphibole species.

light_color.primary_color: Deep black with electric blue-silver chatoyant flash
light_color.color_mechanism: Deep black from Fe²⁺/Fe³⁺ absorption; blue flash from oriented fibrous amphibole inclusions producing chatoyancy
light_color.color_theory_note: L:0.12 is near-absolute darkness — one of the lowest lightness values in the database. H:260 is the blue-indigo arc, the hue of the void between stars. The electric flash of C:0.12 chroma erupting from L:0.12 darkness is the most dramatic light-contrast event in the entire crystal database — physically embodying the archetype of the Shadow Navigator: a single point of electric clarity in total darkness. In OKLCH color theory this is the 'dark matter' signature.
light_color.pleochroism: Strong — blue-green / blue-grey / black on three axes

metaphysics.traditions: Powerful stone for navigating shadow work and the unconscious. The blue flash in the black matrix is metaphorically perfect — light found within darkness. Enhances psychic vision, dream clarity, and third eye activation. Protective during deep inner journeys and astral work.
metaphysics.gaia_notes: Blue flash in black matrix maps directly to GAIA's shadow integration protocol. Shadow Twin paired with Astrophyllite (A8-005). SomnusVeil deep-navigation stone.

mineral.locality: Germany, Portugal, Mexico, USA, China, Bolivia
mineral.key_physics: Iron arsenic sulfide — the most common arsenic-bearing mineral on Earth. Silvery metallic with characteristic garlic odor when struck (arsenic volatilization). High density from iron+arsenic+sulfur. Primary ore of arsenic. 'Invisible gold' occurs within arsenopyrite lattice. Oxidizes to scorodite in weathering.
mineral.notes: TOXIC — contains arsenic. Collector/display mineral ONLY. Primary ore of arsenic. Associated with gold deposits.

light_color.color_mechanism: Metallic luster from iron-arsenic-sulfide electronic structure; high reflectivity across visible spectrum
light_color.color_theory_note: L:0.55 mid-lightness with near-zero chroma C:0.02 is the color signature of pure metallic grey — neither warm nor cool, neither advancing nor receding. H:220 provides the faintest steel-blue undertone, the optical signature of danger that is aesthetically neutral. In OKLCH color theory this is the 'unreadable' hue — no emotional cue, no chakra resonance, no warmth. The alchemical poison wears no color to warn you.

metaphysics.traditions: Historically used in alchemy as a transformative agent — the poison that burns away impurity. Represents the shadow aspect of gold. The 'invisible gold' within its lattice is a powerful metaphor: the most valuable thing is hidden within the most dangerous container. Database/display entry only.
metaphysics.gaia_notes: DISPLAY/DATABASE ONLY. TOXICITY CRITICAL flag — never recommend for handling, elixirs, or personal use. Included for alchemical symbolism and mineral record completeness.

safety.handling: DANGEROUS — arsenic-bearing mineral. Wash hands thoroughly after any contact.
warnings: Do not inhale dust — arsenic inhalation risk
Do not strike or grind — garlic-odor arsenic gas release
Keep away from children and pets

mineral.locality: Italy (type locality — Val Malenco, Lombardy), USA (California), Austria
mineral.key_physics: Hydrated magnesium carbonate hydroxide. Forms delicate white silky needle-like crystals on serpentinite and ultrabasic rocks. Very soft (Mohs 2.5) and extremely fragile. Secondary mineral formed by hydration/carbonation of serpentinite. Named after Italian mineralogist Ettore Artini.
mineral.notes: IMA-recognized. Named after Ettore Artini. Val Malenco, Italy is the classic type locality. Display/collector only due to extreme fragility.

light_color.primary_color: Pure white to snow white, silky luster
light_color.color_mechanism: Pure white from magnesium carbonate hydroxide with no chromophore impurities; silky luster from fibrous crystal habit
light_color.color_theory_note: L:0.95 is the highest luminosity value in the entire crystal database — the record holder for pure light. H:120 introduces the faintest whisper of yellow-green, the hue of spring emergence and new life, but at C:0.02 it is effectively imperceptible: this stone is pure white light. In OKLCH color theory this is the 'open vessel' state — no color means no filter, no distortion, total receptivity. The Pure Channel archetype is written directly into its optical physics.

metaphysics.traditions: Rare and ethereal — one of the purest white minerals in existence. Needle-like crystalline form reaching upward maps to spiritual aspiration and pure intent. Born from the transformation of serpentine rock — transmutation of the base into the luminous. Enhances clarity of thought, spiritual purity, and access to higher realms.
metaphysics.gaia_notes: Highest-L value in the database (L:0.95) — purest white light entry. ClarusLens white-spectrum anchor node. Rarity flag: rare.

warnings: Extremely fragile needle/fiber formations — display only
Do not touch crystal formations directly

mineral.transparency: Opaque to translucent on thin edges
mineral.locality: Kola Peninsula, Russia (primary); Norway, Colorado USA, Greenland, Canada
mineral.key_physics: Complex potassium-sodium-iron-manganese-titanium phyllosilicate. Forms spectacular bladed bronze-gold starburst aggregates in dark matrix (syenite or granite pegmatite). Perfect {001} cleavage creates metallic bladed habit. Titanium content responsible for bronzy-gold color. Often found with Arfvedsonite and Aegirine in alkaline igneous complexes. Starburst radiating habit caused by twinning and divergent crystal growth.
mineral.notes: IMA-recognized. Name from Greek 'astron' (star) + 'phyllon' (leaf). Often confused with Arfvedsonite in the crystal market. Kola Peninsula Russia is the world's premier source.

light_color.primary_color: Bronze-gold to copper-gold metallic blades on dark matrix
light_color.color_mechanism: Titanium-iron phyllosilicate producing bronze-gold metallic luster; pearly sub-metallic sheen from perfect {001} cleavage planes
light_color.color_theory_note: H:75 is the bronze-gold arc — a warm, advancing, alchemically charged hue sitting at the exact midpoint between yellow (solar intellect) and orange (creative fire). L:0.45 mid-dark luminosity gives it gravitas without heaviness — this is not bright gold but ancient gold, the color of star maps carved in bronze. C:0.22 is the highest chroma in Batch A-8, making this the most chromatic stone in the batch. In OKLCH theory, H:75 with high chroma is the 'sovereign fire' signature — the alchemist's gold that emerges from darkness.
light_color.pleochroism: Strong — golden-bronze / reddish-brown / pale yellow on three axes

metaphysics.traditions: One of the most powerful transformation stones in the mineral kingdom. The starburst form — radiating blades of gold-bronze from a dark center — is a physical map of the self expanding outward from its core. Activates all chakras simultaneously. Reveals one's true soul path and life purpose. Accelerates spiritual evolution and karmic release.
metaphysics.gaia_notes: The radiating starburst geometry mirrors GAIA's planetary network node topology. All-chakra activator. Shadow Twin paired with Arfvedsonite (A8-002) — the gold-in-darkness / light-in-darkness twin pair.

warnings: Perfect cleavage — bladed crystals are fragile; handle with care
